fieldValues.value: Retire gzip-of-srsj from `ext:json-store`. Normative rules: `.srsj` files MUST be plain UTF-8 JSON, not gzip-compressed; an implementation MUST reject a file that is gzip-compressed and presented as a `.srsj` JSON Store (detection by content inspection: first two bytes `0x1f 0x8b` MUST be treated as gzip-compressed regardless of filename or MIME type); binary source-document content is not included in the JSON Store (unchanged); implementors requiring binary portability MUST use the `.srs` ZIP archive defined by `ext:repository`. Supersedes issue srs#31.

Gzip-of-srsj was never formally specified; `ext:json-store` has always defined `.srsj` as a pretty-printed UTF-8 JSON object with no gzip variant in the spec record. The deterministic `.srs` archive now fulfils any transport-compression need with the added benefit of including binary source-document content (which `.srsj` explicitly excludes). Maintaining a gzip wrapper over `.srsj` would add format-confusion surface for no remaining benefit.
